rationale: The A/B framing conflated two separable layers: WHERE plateau is detected+routed (orchestration, loop-owned, reuses score_stall_gate + spike_attempted guard — Option A correct here) and WHAT performs the targeted rewrite (a cross-cutting capability: autodev/rn-remediate/refine-to-ready all independently avoid the same non-additive rewrite of Implementation Steps/AC/Files-to-Modify). An inline autodev state (A) makes the capability non-reusable and non-user-invocable; a refine-issue flag (B) drags in the wrong additive-research contract and a 7-site blast radius. A skill is the composable, user-invocable home (per CLAUDE.md 'Prefer Skills over Agents'). ready-issue overlap check: adjacent but wrong home (issue↔codebase truth source vs issue↔itself; would never fire on the plateau; must not run on every issue) — but its CORRECTIONS_MADE ledger + verdict + append-log output contract is the precedent the new skill mirrors for auditability.
rule: Option C: split — autodev.yaml keeps plateau detection (Option A's orchestration half); the targeted reconcile rewrite becomes a new /ll:reconcile-issue skill called from the reconcile_current state and independently user-invocable.
alternatives_rejected: Option A (reconcile logic inlined in autodev state — not reusable across loops, not user-invocable); Option B (--reconcile flag on /ll:refine-issue — wrong contract, 7+ call sites with 'bulldoze' warnings); reconcile as a mode of /ll:ready-issue (rejected: reconciles issue↔codebase not issue↔itself, invisible to its triggers, runs on every issue).
